A.label: Fast lane
A.desc: Small, fully GPU-resident, sub-50 ms latency

B.label: Balanced
B.desc: Fits in 16 GB VRAM, the workhorse zone

C.label: Quality
C.desc: CPU offload, slow but highest accuracy

models.name: Qwen3 4B (2507)
models.note: Best small agentic model. Solid ES, Catalan acceptable.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B
models.note: Best Catalan support of the small models. Weaker function calling than Qwen3.

models.name: Phi-4 Mini
models.note: Speed king in the small tier, but multilingual gap rules it out for ES/CA.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.note: Dominated. 2026 community testing reports unreliable triage / hallucinated replies.

models.name: Qwen3.5 9B
models.note: Confirmed 250k+ context on this exact GPU. Loses to Gemma 4 26B MoE on speed AND quality.

models.name: Gemma 3 12B
models.note: Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE on every axis.

models.name: Mistral Small 3.2
models.note: Strong European multilingual but dominated on speed by MoE alternatives.

models.name: GPT-OSS 20B
models.note: Excellent BFCL/τ-bench, native MXFP4 fits 16 GB. Multilingual ES/CA unverified — test before relying.

models.name: Gemma 4 26B MoE
models.primary_use: ★ Default workhorse — transcripts, documents, deep agentic, EN↔ES↔CA
models.note: The standout 16 GB-class model of 2026. 99 t/s confirmed on RTX 5060 Ti at full 256k context.

models.name: Gemma 3 27B (dense)
models.note: Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE: same family, higher quality, ~6× faster decode thanks to MoE.

models.name: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B
models.note: Slight VRAM overflow (~2 GB) but still fast. Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE at this speed/quality combo.

models.name: Qwen3 32B (dense)
models.primary_use: Quality runs, hard agentic, batch overnight
models.note: BFCL v3 leader among open weights (75.7%). ~3.5 GB CPU spill, ~10–12 t/s.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B
models.note: Heavy CPU offload (24 GB+ in RAM). Dominated by Qwen3 32B: faster AND higher quality.

asr_models.name: faster-whisper Turbo
asr_models.tagline: ★ Drop-in upgrade from your current Turbo
asr_models.note: Same Whisper Large v3 Turbo weights you already use, reimplemented in CTranslate2. ~2× faster, lower VRAM via INT8. No quality regression. Use compute_type="int8_float16".

asr_models.name: Whisper Large v3 Turbo
asr_models.note: Solid for EN/ES transcription. Switch the runtime to faster-whisper for free speed; keep the model.

asr_models.name: faster-whisper Large v3
asr_models.tagline: ☆ Use for ES → EN single-pass translation
asr_models.note: Higher accuracy than Turbo and supports task=translate. Fallback for workload G when the LLM slot is busy.

asr_models.name: IBM Granite Speech 3.3 8B
asr_models.tagline: ☆ Accuracy upgrade if you hit Turbo ceiling
asr_models.note: 5.85% avg WER — second only to Canary-Qwen on the HF leaderboard. Real ES support but per-language ES benchmarks sparse. Heavier (~5 GB, ~3× the latency). Worth it only if Turbo is making errors that matter.

asr_models.name: Qwen3-ASR
asr_models.note: Released January 2026 by Alibaba. Per-language ES WER not yet published. Could displace Turbo if benchmarks land favorably.

asr_models.name: NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B
asr_models.note: #1 on HF Open ASR Leaderboard at 5.63% WER, but English-strong with very limited Spanish support.

asr_translation_paths.label: Architecture (i) — Single-model
asr_translation_paths.headline: faster-whisper Large v3 with task=translate
asr_translation_paths.pros: Simplest pipeline, ~2 GB VRAM, ~30× realtime, single pass
asr_translation_paths.cons: Literal English output. No style polish, no summarization, occasional idiom loss.
asr_translation_paths.verdict: Fallback when LLM slot is occupied

asr_translation_paths.label: Architecture (ii) — Two-stage
asr_translation_paths.headline: faster-whisper Turbo (ES) → Gemma 4 26B MoE (translate + polish + disfluency)
asr_translation_paths.pros: Higher quality, context-aware translation, polishing + filler removal in same LLM call. Catalan extension comes free.
asr_translation_paths.cons: ~15 GB VRAM total — fast-lane model drops out for batches.

disfluency_verdict.decision: Fold into the LLM polishing pass
disfluency_verdict.reasoning: Specialized disfluency-removal models (DisfluencyFixer, DISCO) are research-grade and primarily EN/HI/DE/FR — Spanish coverage is sparse. Tier-B LLMs (Gemma 4 26B MoE, Qwen3.5 9B) handle disfluency removal extremely well as part of an instruction-following pass. Adding a separate component means another model loaded, another VRAM slot, another point of failure, and worse multilingual coverage than the LLM you already run.
disfluency_verdict.prompt:
Clean up this transcript:
- Remove filler words (um, uh, eh, este, like, you know)
- Remove false starts and self-corrections, keep the corrected version
- Tighten conciseness without losing meaning or voice
- Preserve technical terms and proper nouns exactly
- Keep the output in [Spanish/English] — do not translate

Transcript:
{transcript}

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 1 — Two lanes
concurrency_recipes.note: Both at full speed. Total ~17 GB — minor shared-memory spill, handled gracefully on Blackwell.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 2 — Quality batch
concurrency_recipes.summary: Qwen3 4B (GPU) + Qwen3 32B dense (GPU + ~3.5 GB CPU spill).
concurrency_recipes.note: Fast lane unaffected. 32B at ~10 t/s — overnight transcript reprocessing.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 3 — Three concurrent
concurrency_recipes.note: The 7800X3D's 96 MB L3 makes Gemma 3 4B on CPU run ~10 t/s — useful as a translation specialist.

top_picks.why: Best small agentic model on this hardware. Pair with Gemma 3 4B if Catalan polish matters more than tool-call accuracy.
99 t/s, full 256k context, Google-grade multilingual. The default for transcript polishing, document work, and deep agentic.
BFCL leader at 75.7%. ~10 t/s with mild CPU spill — reserve for batch quality runs where correctness > latency.

agentic_light.reason: Strict frontier reading. Qwen3 4B is the Tier A top pick on the chart (Apache 2.0, ~3 GB Q4_K_M, ~100 t/s, best small agentic model). Gemma 4 E4B is the incumbent and works fine, but it isn't plotted on the frontier — Qwen3 4B is the analyzed Pareto pick for OpenClaw routing/classification.

agentic_heavy.reason: Incumbent and Tier B top pick on the frontier. 99 t/s, 256k ctx, strong multilingual including Catalan. The standout 16 GB-class model of 2026 — no swap warranted.

audio_transcribe.reason: Runtime upgrade only — same Turbo weights via CTranslate2 give ~2× RTFx and lower VRAM (report §7.3 ★ Primary). Engine code change is manual.

audio_translate.reason: Strict frontier reading. Report §7.4 picks two-stage (Turbo → Gemma 4 26B MoE) as the default for ES→EN, leaving whisper-medium as a fallback only when the LLM slot is busy. Mark as watch — keep the slot defined and lazy-loaded, but don't actively maintain it.
